description: Open-ended performance investigation. The consolidated Talk rework (545ba14) added genuine reliability but at a measurable cost — tab-switch and scroll lag, and at least one crash/reload. Goal: identify the hot spots and decide which features to keep, tune, or back off.

items.before: Talk pane feels noticeably slower since the consolidated rework. Symptoms: tab-switch lag, scroll jank, an unexplained crash-and-reload. Suspects (none confirmed): (1) `pendingPoll` adds 1 fetch/sec + a 64KB JSONL read in Rust each call; (2) `lastJsonl` buffer adds a reactive hop; (3) ref-preservation in `sessionTurns` walks the previous turns each parse; (4) `<Items data="{turns.slice(-100)}">` regenerates a 100-element array per evaluation; (5) `scrollAllToBottom` iterates `document.querySelectorAll('*')` on every `turns.length` change; (6) three concurrent DataSources (sessionMeta, pendingPoll, latestSession) each with `Cache-Control: no-store`.
items.after:
Step 1 — baseline measurement. Add temporary instrumentation: log time spent in `sessionTurns`, `read_latest_session_pending` (Rust), and the Items render path. Capture during normal use (idle, typing, agent activity, tab switching). Use the existing `logToHost` channel so logs land in cargo run stderr without polluting Talk.

Step 2 — interpret. Identify which work dominates. Likely candidates: Markdown re-render across ~100 turns on every poll cycle, or the per-tick reactivity cascade. If it's the Markdown, no amount of polling tuning will help — the structural fix is windowed/virtualized rendering. If it's the polling, dial it back.

Step 3 — selective rollback or tuning. Concrete knobs (smallest-impact first):
  • Drop `turnLimit` from 100 to 50 (one-line; halves render cost).
  • Cache the scroll-target element in helpers.js so the `turns.length` listener doesn't `querySelectorAll('*')` every time.
  • Reduce pendingPoll to 2s (still much better than no menu).
  • Reduce pendingPoll's server-side read from 64KB to 4-8KB.
  • Remove the `lastJsonl` buffer and accept brief refetch-null states (the tail endpoint may have made the buffer unnecessary).
  • As a last resort: roll back the modal (revert to inline-no-expand, click → modal stays).

Step 4 — possibly add windowing/virtualization for >50 turns if Markdown is the bottleneck. That's a bigger change and would warrant its own worklist item.

Acceptable outcome: Talk feels responsive on tab-switch and scroll, with menu detection within 1-2s. Honest about tradeoffs in the resulting commit message.
